suites.name: E2E — fast tiers (Playwright + BDD, Docker)
suites.command: npm run test:teardown >/dev/null 2>&1; npm run test:setup && { npm run test:ci; ec=$?; }; npm run test:teardown; exit ${ec:-1}

suites.name: Backend integration (.NET, needs Postgres)
suites.command: docker compose up -d --wait postgres && dotnet test Modelibr.sln --filter "Category=Integration"
suites.note: Starts the dev Postgres container automatically (left running — it's the dev database).

suites.name: Backup/restore E2E (Docker)
suites.command: npm run test:teardown >/dev/null 2>&1; npm run test:full

suites.name: Storybook visual regression (Playwright)
suites.command: npm run build-storybook && npm run test-storybook
suites.note: Needs Playwright chromium installed (npx playwright install chromium).

ci.note: Storybook is built in CI, but the visual snapshot test is not run

suites.name: E2E — performance / throughput (Docker)
suites.command: npm run test:teardown >/dev/null 2>&1; npm run test:setup && { npm run test:performance; ec=$?; }; npm run test:teardown; exit ${ec:-1}

results.name: Backend integration (.NET, needs Postgres)
results.note: Starts the dev Postgres container automatically (left running — it's the dev database).

results.name: Storybook visual regression (Playwright)
results.note: Needs Playwright chromium installed (npx playwright install chromium).

scenarios.name: Merge ORM packed texture using Split Channels
scenarios.comment:
Note: Additional scenarios for Albedo merge and override warnings are
temporarily disabled due to backend constraint IX_Textures_FileId_TextureType_SourceChannel
preventing the same file content from being used in multiple texture sets.
This requires a backend fix to allow TextureSet-scoped uniqueness.

Height card shows mode dropdown

features.title: Shader-based Channel Extraction in 3D Viewer
features.description: Verify that channel-packed textures (ORM) display correctly in the 3D viewer with individual channels extracted to their respective material slots. Background: Given I am on the texture sets page

scenarios.name: ORM packed texture displays with channel extraction
Texture set textures are applied to 3D model

features.title: Texture Set Kind (Multi-Model vs Global Materials)
features.description: Tests for the texture set kind feature including: - Kind filter tabs (Multi-Model and Global Materials) - Default tab is Global Materials - Creating texture sets with specific kind - Changing kind via API - Drag and drop between kind tabs - Thumbnail auto-generation on kind change to Universal - Context menu Regenerate Thumbnail visibility - Kind persistence, API filtering, and global texture files

scenarios.name: Default tab is Global Materials
Creating texture sets places them in correct kind tabs
Changing kind between Multi-Model and Universal via API
Drag and drop between kind tabs
Thumbnail auto-generated when kind changes to Universal
Context menu Regenerate Thumbnail visibility depends on kind
Regenerate Thumbnail via context menu
Kind tab switch never surfaces the previous tab's data
Kind persistence, API filtering, and global texture files

features.title: EXR Texture Preview Loading
features.description: Verify that texture sets containing EXR textures can be previewed in the 3D viewer without errors. EXR textures are loaded natively using the EXRLoader with proper color space handling. Preview tab is only available for Universal (Global Materials) texture sets.

scenarios.name: Preview tab renders without errors when texture set contains EXR textures
Preview tab renders with mixed EXR and standard textures

features.title: Texture Set Thumbnail with Mixed Image Formats
features.description: Verifies that texture set thumbnail generation succeeds when the set contains a mix of standard images (JPEG, PNG) and EXR files. Previously, EXR files caused the Puppeteer-based renderer to crash with "Target closed" errors because EXR binary data was sent as base64 JPEG to the browser, which cannot decode it. Also verifies that all texture types (including Displacement, Bump, Alpha) are correctly mapped to Three.js material properties.

scenarios.name: Thumbnail generated for texture set with mixed PNG and EXR textures

features.title: Auto-generated Thumbnail Previews
features.description: Thumbnails should be auto-generated on file upload. Texture files should have 4 thumbnails (RGB, R, G, B). Sprite files should have 1 thumbnail (RGB). All preview surfaces should use thumbnails instead of raw files.

scenarios.name: Texture PNG file generates RGB and per-channel previews
EXR and sprite files generate correct previews
Texture set surfaces show thumbnail previews
Sprites page shows thumbnail previews

features.title: Preset (Variant) Workflow
features.description: Tests the full preset workflow: adding a new preset, linking a texture set to it, selecting it for preview, and setting it as main. Background: Given the following models exist in shared state: | name                 | | single-version-model | And the following texture sets exist in shared state: | name        | | blue_color  | | red_color   |

scenarios.name: Add preset, link texture, set as main

features.title: Preset Lifecycle, Texture Association, and Main Variant Thumbnails
features.description: Comprehensive tests for preset (variant) management: - Creating, persisting, and deleting presets - Linking/unlinking texture sets per preset - Preset survives when last texture set is unlinked (Bug #1 fix) - Switching presets shows correct texture sets (Bug #3 fix) - Setting main variant and thumbnail regeneration (Bug #2 fix) Background: Given the following models exist in shared state: | name                 | | single-version-model | And the following texture sets exist in shared state: | name        | | blue_color  | | red_color   |

scenarios.name: Create a new preset via UI
Preset persists after navigating away and back
Delete a preset removes it from dropdown
Link texture set to a preset and verify it appears
Unlink last texture set from a preset — preset still visible (Bug #1)
Two presets show different texture sets when switching (Bug #3)
Unlink texture set from a specific material
Set a preset as main variant
Setting main variant triggers thumbnail regeneration with variant textures (Bug #2)
Deleting a preset cleans up its texture mappings

features.title: Embedded Materials Preset
features.description: Tests the built-in "Embedded" preset that preserves the model's original PBR materials. Verifies UI behavior: preset selection, "Embedded" label on unlinked materials, hidden Link Texture Set buttons, and the ability to set Embedded as the main variant. Background: Given the following models exist in shared state: | name                 | | single-version-model |

scenarios.name: Embedded preset appears in dropdown
scenarios.comment: ── Embedded preset is always available in the dropdown ─────────────

scenarios.name: Embedded preset hides Link Texture Set buttons
Switching from Embedded to Default restores Link buttons
Set Embedded as main variant
Embedded as main persists after navigation
Restore Default as main variant after tests

features.title: TIFF Texture Preview Loading
features.description: Verify that texture sets containing TIFF textures can be previewed in the 3D viewer without errors. Browsers cannot decode TIFF natively; textures are routed through utif2 and re-wrapped as Three.js textures.

scenarios.name: Preview tab renders without errors when texture set contains a TIFF texture
Preview tab renders with mixed TIFF and standard textures

features.title: Convert Texture Set Kind from the Materials Panel
features.description: A texture set linked to a model can be converted between Multi-Model and Single Model kinds via the right-click context menu on its materials-item. Converting a Multi-Model set that is shared with other models warns the user and unlinks it from those models. Background: Given the following models exist in shared state: | name                 | | single-version-model | | multi-version-model  |

scenarios.name: Context menu offers Single Model conversion for a Multi-Model set
Context menu offers Multi-Model conversion for a Single Model set
Convert a Multi-Model texture set to Single Model
Convert a Single Model texture set back to Multi-Model
Converting a shared texture set warns and unlinks the other model

features.title: Texture Set toolbar search
features.description: Coverage trap for the shared list-toolbar's debounced server-side search on the Texture Sets page. These scenarios exist to catch the classes of regressions that previously slipped through: - Mid-typing re-renders that unmount the search input and drop focus (the bug that motivated, and then unmotivated, the `placeholderData` workaround on the list query). - Search-name filtering moving from client-side to server-side without a test asserting the server actually narrowed the results.

scenarios.name: Search input retains focus across debounce/refetch cycle
Toolbar search narrows the texture-set grid by name

features.title: Texture Set Categories (per-kind)
features.description: Categories are scoped per texture-set kind: Global Materials (Universal) and Multi-Model (ModelSpecific) keep separate category pools. A category created for one kind must not appear for the other, sets can be assigned to a category of their own kind, and the grid can be filtered by it.

scenarios.name: A category created for Multi-Model is not shared with Global Materials
A category created for Global Materials is not shared with Multi-Model
Assign and filter a Multi-Model texture set by category
Assign and filter a Global Materials texture set by category
Rename a texture set category
Renaming a Multi-Model category to a name used by Global Materials is allowed
Renaming a category to a duplicate name in the same kind is rejected
The category manager surfaces a save error when the rename collides
Delete a texture set category

features.title: Version Switching
features.description: Background: Given the following models exist in shared state: | name                | | multi-version-model |

scenarios.name: Version dropdown shows all versions with thumbnails
scenarios.comment:
@slow: depends on both version thumbnails being Ready in the DB, which
races with the asset-processor queue. GitHub-hosted PR-CI runners are
too slow / variable for this to be reliable in the fast/parallel
chromium phase; this test runs locally (node run-e2e.js) and in the
nightly slow-tests workflow, both of which run @slow sequentially.

scenarios.name: Switching versions updates the viewer

features.title: Sessions section restores closed windows
features.description: The New Tab page surfaces a "Sessions" section that lists every archived (closed) browser window — distinct from the per-tab "Recently Closed" list. Each entry shows the tab count per panel, restores all tabs into the current window on click, and persists across reloads.

features.title: Shared File Protection
features.description: Tests that hash-based file deduplication protects shared files during deletion. When two models share the same physical file (same SHA256 hash), deleting one model should not destroy the file used by the other model.

scenarios.name: Permanently deleting one model does not destroy files used by another

Theme persists across reload

SSL Certificate section exposes a download link

features.title: Thumbnail rendering honors live settings
features.description: The asset-processor must refresh its render config from the backend before every job. These scenarios change the thumbnail size, trigger a bulk regeneration, wait for the worker to actually re-render, and then read the PNG IHDR off the served file to prove the on-disk dimensions match the saved setting. This is the integration-level guarantee that the cheaper unit tests can't make on their own. Background: Given settings are reset to defaults via API And at least one model with a thumbnail exists

scenarios.name: A new thumbnail size produces files at that size
Frame count is read live from settings per job

features.title: Environment map list parity
features.description: Environment maps should match the current model-style list interactions.

scenarios.name: Drag and drop upload updates the toolbar count and keeps the detected 4K label
scenarios.comment:
Both scenarios upload an environment map and open its viewer, which triggers
PMREM/cube preview generation. On slow PR-CI runners this races with other
parallel thumbnail jobs; @slow runs sequentially in the slow phase.

scenarios.name: Drag and drop HDR upload keeps the inferred 2K label

features.title: Cube environment maps and preview loading
features.description: Users can upload cube textures with explicit face mapping and inspect them in the viewer.

scenarios.name: Upload a cube environment map with a custom thumbnail and preview it
scenarios.comment:
Uploads 6 cube faces + a custom thumbnail, then opens the viewer which
triggers PMREM cubemap generation. On slow PR-CI runners this races
with other parallel thumbnail jobs; @slow runs sequentially.
